title: He Who Would Valiant Be

metadata.composer: 'Monks Gate' traditional English Ralph Vaughan Williams, 1904
metadata.lyricist: John Bunyan, 1684. Modified by Percy Dearmer, 1906
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.
c_lines: Words: John Bunyan, 1684. Modified by Percy Dearmer, 1906.
Music: 'Monks Gate' traditional English.  Setting: Ralph Vaughan Williams, 1904.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.

1.raw_text: He who would valiant be 'gainst all disa- ster, Let him in constancy follow the Master. There's no discouragement shall make him once relent His first avowed intent to be a pilgrim.

2.raw_text: Who so beset him round with dismal stories Do but themselves confound his strength the more is. No foes shall stay his might; though he with giants fight, He will make good his right to be a pilgrim.

3.raw_text: Since, Lord, Thou dost defend us with Thy Spirit, We know we at the end, shall life inherit. Then fancies flee away! I'll fear not what men say, I'll labor night and day to be a pilgrim.
